Noticed elevated temperature at combiner box CB-03-07 during thermal scan at 11:25, ambient 30°C; IR camera recorded junction temperature 72°C (baseline 48°C) on busbar lug connecting string group S-78. Measured contact resistance across lug with four-wire method: 0.012 Ω, up from baseline 0.0048 Ω which indicates increased contact resistance likely from oxidation; visible greenish corrosion on lug assembly. Retorqued lug to 35 Nm and applied contact cleaner and anti-oxidant paste PN CONCT-01; recommended replacement of lug and full re-torque of combiner box within 14 days and additional thermal scan after replacement. Logged CB serial CB-03 and took photos for asset file.

Observed progressive drop in output from string S-402 during routine performance check, measured current fell from baseline 10.4 A to 8.1 A (22% loss) at 09:45 with irradiance 900 W/m²; I-V sweep run on string shows reduced Isc and slight knee flattening. Insulation resistance test on that string returned 18 MΩ falling below typical >50 MΩ baseline which suggests early moisture ingress or PID onset; visual inspection found soiling and early browning on modules M402-1 through M402-18 and partial shading from newly grown sapling at row edge. Cleaned modules and applied anti-reflective wash, trimmed vegetation to clear 3 m path, and flagged string for detailed IEC-compliant IR and PID diagnostic within 7 days; recommended monitoring skew in SCADA for further degradation.

Emergency callout to Greenfield Solar Array combiner field CB-07-03 after SCADA reported arcing alarm and loss of feeder; arrived on site at 16:12 under light rain, observed visible smoke and burnt insulation odor. Thermal probe recorded hotspot 120°C at busbar junction and clamp housing melted; measured AC feeder tripped and DC isolator in OFF position; took immediate action to isolate combiner, lock-out/tag-out per safety SOP, and coordinated with operations to open AC service disconnect at PCC. Collected failed components (burned fuse FU-07-3 and busbar lug P/N LUG-25-B) and placed site under fire watch; recommended urgent replacement of combiner box, full electrical continuity and insulation tests, and an investigation into moisture ingress and failed overcurrent protection before re-energizing. Incident logged and emailed priority escalation to maintenance manager and insurance.

Performed grounding and earthing verification across southern inverter pad at 07:40 in wet ground conditions; measured earth rod resistance 0.9 Ω using clamp-on ground tester which meets site acceptance criteria (<1.0 Ω). Conducted continuity checks between module frames and AC ground bus, found one ground clamp on row 5 loose (identified as CL-5G-02), torqued to 30 Nm and applied corrosion inhibitor GEL-88 to clamp interface. Documented pull-test results and updated asset ledger with clamp serial CL-5G-02 and inspector initials; no further action required.

Replaced irradiance sensor (pyranometer PN PYR-01, serial PYR-7842) at weather mast 2 after sensor drift flagged by monitoring; swap performed at 12:05 on a clear day, aligned sensor level within 0.5° and secured with anti-corrosion bolts PN M6-SS. Post-installation checks show irradiance reading 920 W/m² consistent with handheld reference Kipp & Zonen unit, module Vmp and Isc sample on adjacent string matched expected curve within 4%. Calibrated new sensor in SCADA, logged calibration offset and old unit removed for return to vendor under RMA #RMA-2209. Left site tidy, noted torque values and serials in maintenance record.

I performed the quarterly hoist and trolley inspection on the north gantry crane at Northgate Freight Terminal (50 t rated). Load test with a 25 t test weight was performed, hoist brake holding torque measured 62 N·m (within spec 55-70 N·m). Replaced worn wire rope clip (PN-CRN-4521) and applied fresh mobilgrease EP2 to sheave bearings (SKF 6311-2RS). Ambient 8°C, overcast, access via yard 4; crane taken back to service after functional tests and zero drift observed in hoist encoder.

Inspected turnout assembly on Track 3 (nearest loading bay), measured rail gauge at 1432 mm (nominal 1435 mm) and noted slight lateral movement on the outside stock rail; all Pandrol e-clips present and torques checked at 200 N·m with calibrated torque wrench. Replaced two corroded railbed bolts with M24 stainless bolts (PN-BT-M24-SS) and tamped ballast beneath sleeper #72 to restore alignment by 5 mm. Performed ultrasonic flaw sweep on switch blade with GRT UTS-100, no internal defects detected. Weather sunny, yard busy but clear access, track returned to normal operation.

Visited wayside signal cabinet S-07 to replace backup battery (12 V 35 Ah, PN-BAT-12V35) and verify track circuit continuity for the east approach; DC resistance measured 0.72 Ω across rails, within acceptable range. Re-seated relay module (Siemens RLY-SM24) and cleaned corrosion on terminal strip with contact cleaner; LED indicator green and track occupancy tests passed. Night shift reported no further signal dropouts after works completed at 22:15 under light drizzle.

Performed scheduled maintenance on stormwater sump pump near southern yard (Pump model: Grundfos NB 40-160, PN-PMP-GF-NB40) after heavy rainfall last week; replaced mechanical seal kit (PN-SEAL-MK8) and inspected impeller for debris. Measured flow at discharge at 420 L/min (spec 400-450 L/min) and system pressure stable at 1.1 bar. Cleared access hatch and logged new maintenance interval of 180 days; technician noted no evidence of oil ingress and pump vibration within 3 mm/s.

Replaced two LED floodlight drivers on yard lighting pole 14 (Philips Xitanium PN-LD-058) after dimming reported by operations; inrush current measured 1.2 A and output voltage stable at 54 Vdc. Inspected associated pole-mounted transformer (480 V to 240 V, T-TRF-240/480) and found primary oil level normal, thermistor temp 42°C under clear skies at 19:30. Restored lighting to full intensity and logged parts into inventory for next scheduled replacement cycle.

During routine track geometry checks on Track 2 I identified a hairline crack in the weld at the heel of the frog assembly adjacent to switch 2 (approx 120 mm length, ~1.2 mm opening on surface). Ultrasonic reading (GRT UTS-100) returned a discontinuity at 18 mm depth; recommend restricting speeds to 15 km/h over turnout and scheduling weld repair within 72 hours. Temporarily marked area with orange flagging and notified operations control; photo and GIS tag uploaded to asset record PN-FRG-HTL-02.

Observed elevated vibration and bearing temperature on gantry crane trolley bearing during afternoon shift (bearing SKF 22224 E, PN-SKF-22224). Vibration sensor recorded 6.4 mm/s RMS (warning threshold 5.0 mm/s) and IR gun showed bearing housing at 78°C (ambient 25°C), indicating early wear or lubricant degradation. Lubricated bearing with Mobilgrease XHP and scheduled bearing replacement within 14 days using spare PN-SKF-22224 from stores; placed crane on reduced duty limit to 30 t until replacement completed.

Emergency callout at 03:45 for flood washout under Track 1 near the north apron caused by a ruptured 200 mm water main; ballast scour measured up to 350 mm depth under sleeper 18-22 and track vertical settlement approx 120 mm over a 4 m length, rendering the track unsafe. Trains suspended on all six tracks and site cordoned with temporary shoring (timber braces + hydraulic jacks) installed to prevent further collapse; requested 50 t crane mobilization (unit rated 50 t on site) to lift derated rail panels for emergency reinstatement. Coordinated with utility crew to isolate main and begin pipe repair, and raised urgent work order for full ballast replacement and welded rail joint re-profile; safety officer signed stop-work until shoring approved.
